steps.title: Use Desmos to fit the exponential table
steps.content: This table is exponential, so Desmos can find the model directly. Enter the data as $x_1=[0,1,2]$ and $y_1=[670,674.02,678.06]$, then type $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$. Desmos gives $a=670$ and $b=1.006$, so the graphable equation is $y=670(1.006)^x$.

steps.title: Translate back to the answer choices
steps.content: The question uses $d$ and $t$ instead of $y$ and $x$, so $y=670(1.006)^x$ becomes $d=670(1.006)^t$. Since $1.006=1+0.006$, the same equation is $d=670(1+0.006)^t$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
